archetype: Swiss & High-Contrast (Archetype 4) mixed with Enterprise Premium

spacing_and_layout.alignment: Left-align for dense content, prioritize optical alignment.
spacing_and_layout.navigation: Sticky sidebar navigation for desktop, with active states clearly marked (e.g., bold text, light background fill like slate-100, and primary color text). Mobile uses a clean hamburger menu.

cards.style: Subtle roundness (rounded-lg max 8px), flat white background, 1px border-slate-200, shadow-sm. No heavy shadows. Hover state: slight lift (-translate-y-[1px]) and shadow-md.

buttons.primary: bg-[#1E3A5F] text-white hover:bg-[#152D4A] rounded-md px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-colors
buttons.secondary: bg-white text-slate-700 border border-slate-200 hover:bg-slate-50 rounded-md px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-colors
buttons.accent: bg-[#0F766E] text-white hover:bg-[#0D635D] rounded-md px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-colors

progress_tracker.style: Linear timeline or bento-grid of session cards. Use sharp, high-contrast markers for completed sessions (e.g., solid #1E3A5F with checkmark). Upcoming sessions muted (text-slate-400, border-slate-200).

resource_hub.style: Grid layout (grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3). Document cards should feel tactile but clean. Include document type icons (lucide-react). Use subtle hover effects to indicate downloadability.

engagement_feed.style: Vertical list format, generous spacing between items. Clean divider lines (border-b border-slate-100) instead of wrapping each item in a card. Minimalist like feature for engagement.

motion.entrance: fade-in and slide-up for lists (staggered if possible)
motion.rules: Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Animations should be micro-interactions, not distracting.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes.
Do not use generic HTML components for complex interactions; use Shadcn equivalents (e.g., Dialog, Select, Tabs).
Maintain high contrast. Do not use pure gray text without a slight blue tint (e.g., use slate over gray).
Ensure the dashboard feels like a premium workspace, like Notion. Use generous padding, subtle borders, and ample whitespace.
Progress tracker should be the visual centerpiece of the participant view.
Do not use heavy shadows or gradients. Stick to flat, clean surfaces with crisp typography.
